---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-12"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```
```{r}
library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

plant_joined 

ui <- page_fillable(
  theme = bs_theme(
    bg = "#e8f4f8",
    fg = "#2c3e50",
    primary = "#FF6B9D",
    "navbar-bg" = "#4A90E2"
  ),
  
  tags$head(
    tags$style(HTML("
      body {
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
        background-attachment: fixed;
      }
      .card {
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95) !important;
        border-radius: 15px !important;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) !important;
      }
      .card-header {
        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #FF6B9D 0%, #C06C84 100%) !important;
        color: white !important;
        font-weight: bold !important;
        border-radius: 15px 15px 0 0 !important;
        padding: 15px !important;
      }
      h2 {
        color: white !important;
        text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
        margin-bottom: 20px;
      }
      .butterfly-emoji {
        font-size: 3em;
        text-align: center;
        margin: 10px 0;
      }
    "))
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "padding: 20px;",
    h2("🦋 Butterfly Breeder App 🦋", style = "text-align: center;"),
    
    layout_columns(
      col_widths = c(3, 9),
      
      # Sidebar with controls
      card(
        card_header("Breeding Controls"),
        div(class = "butterfly-emoji", "🦋"),
        
        sliderInput(
          "nitrogen_range",
          "Nitrogen Level Range (mg/L):",
          min = 0,
          max = 8,
          value = c(0, 8),
          step = 0.1
        ),
        
        checkboxGroupInput(
          "seasons",
          "Select Seasons:",
          choices = c("Spring" = "spring", "Autumn" = "autumn"),
          selected = c("spring", "autumn")
        )
      ),
      
      # Main content area
      div(
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(12),
          
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count vs Larvae Count by Season"),
            plotOutput("main_plot", height = "450px")
          )
        ),
        
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),
          
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count Distribution by Season"),
            plotOutput("egg_distribution", height = "350px")
          ),
          
          card(
            card_header("Larvae Count Distribution by Season"),
            plotOutput("larvae_distribution", height = "350px")
          )
        ),
        
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),
          
          card(
            card_header("Nitrogen Effect on Egg Count"),
            plotOutput("nitrogen_eggs", height = "350px")
          ),
          
          card(
            card_header("Nitrogen Effect on Larvae Count"),
            plotOutput("nitrogen_larvae", height = "350px")
          )
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  # Reactive filtered data
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    plant_joined %>%
      filter(
        season.x %in% input$seasons,
        nitrogen_level >= input$nitrogen_range[1],
        nitrogen_level <= input$nitrogen_range[2]
      )
  })
  
  # Season color palette
  season_colors <- c(
    "spring" = "#90EE90",
    "autumn" = "#D2691E"
  )
  
  # Main plot - Egg vs Larvae relationship
  output$main_plot <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
                 label = "No data available for selected filters",
                 size = 6, color = "gray50") +
        theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, y = larvae_count, color = season.x)) +
        geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
        labs(
          title = "Relationship Between Egg Count and Larvae Count",
          x = "Egg Count",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })
  
  # Egg distribution
  output$egg_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Egg Count Frequency",
        x = "Egg Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Larvae distribution
  output$larvae_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = larvae_count, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Larvae Count Frequency",
        x = "Larvae Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Nitrogen vs Eggs
  output$nitrogen_eggs <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = egg_count, color = season.x)) +
      geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
      scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Nitrogen Impact on Eggs",
        x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
        y = "Egg Count",
        color = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Nitrogen vs Larvae
  output$nitrogen_larvae <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = larvae_count, color = season.x)) +
      geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
      scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Nitrogen Impact on Larvae",
        x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
        y = "Larvae Count",
        color = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

```{r}
library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create sample plant_joined data with spring and autumn seasons
plant_joined 

ui <- page_fillable(
  theme = bs_theme(
    bg = "#e8f4f8",
    fg = "#2c3e50",
    primary = "#FF6B9D",
    "navbar-bg" = "#4A90E2"
  ),
  
  tags$head(
    tags$style(HTML("
      body {
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
        background-attachment: fixed;
      }
      .card {
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95) !important;
        border-radius: 15px !important;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) !important;
      }
      .card-header {
        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #FF6B9D 0%, #C06C84 100%) !important;
        color: white !important;
        font-weight: bold !important;
        border-radius: 15px 15px 0 0 !important;
        padding: 15px !important;
      }
      h2 {
        color: white !important;
        text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
        margin-bottom: 20px;
      }
      .butterfly-emoji {
        font-size: 3em;
        text-align: center;
        margin: 10px 0;
      }
    "))
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "padding: 20px;",
    h2("🦋 Butterfly Breeder App 🦋", style = "text-align: center;"),
    
    layout_columns(
      col_widths = c(3, 9),
      
      # Sidebar with controls
      card(
        card_header("Breeding Controls"),
        div(class = "butterfly-emoji", "🦋"),
        
        sliderInput(
          "nitrogen_range",
          "Nitrogen Level Range (mg/L):",
          min = 0,
          max = 8,
          value = c(0, 8),
          step = 0.1
        ),
        
        checkboxGroupInput(
          "seasons",
          "Select Seasons:",
          choices = c("Spring" = "spring", "Autumn" = "autumn"),
          selected = c("spring", "autumn")
        )
      ),
      
      # Main content area
      div(
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(12),
          
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count vs Larvae Count by Season"),
            plotOutput("main_plot", height = "450px")
          )
        ),
        
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),
          
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count Distribution by Season"),
            plotOutput("egg_distribution", height = "350px")
          ),
          
          card(
            card_header("Larvae Count Distribution by Season"),
            plotOutput("larvae_distribution", height = "350px")
          )
        ),
        
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),
          
          card(
            card_header("Nitrogen Effect on Egg Count"),
            plotOutput("nitrogen_eggs", height = "350px")
          ),
          
          card(
            card_header("Nitrogen Effect on Larvae Count"),
            plotOutput("nitrogen_larvae", height = "350px")
          )
        ),
        
        card(
          card_header("Breeding Summary Statistics"),
          tableOutput("summary_table")
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  # Reactive filtered data
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    plant_joined %>%
      filter(
        season.x %in% input$seasons,
        nitrogen_level >= input$nitrogen_range[1],
        nitrogen_level <= input$nitrogen_range[2]
      )
  })
  
  # Season color palette
  season_colors <- c(
    "spring" = "#90EE90",
    "autumn" = "#D2691E"
  )
  
  # Main plot - Egg vs Larvae relationship
  output$main_plot <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
                 label = "No data available for selected filters",
                 size = 6, color = "gray50") +
        theme_void()
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, y = larvae_count, color = season.x)) +
        geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
        labs(
          title = "Relationship Between Egg Count and Larvae Count",
          x = "Egg Count",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })
  
  # Egg distribution
  output$egg_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Egg Count Frequency",
        x = "Egg Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Larvae distribution
  output$larvae_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = larvae_count, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Larvae Count Frequency",
        x = "Larvae Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Nitrogen vs Eggs
  output$nitrogen_eggs <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = egg_count, color = season.x)) +
      geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
      scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Nitrogen Impact on Eggs",
        x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
        y = "Egg Count",
        color = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Nitrogen vs Larvae
  output$nitrogen_larvae <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = larvae_count, color = season.x)) +
      geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
      scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        title = "Nitrogen Impact on Larvae",
        x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
        y = "Larvae Count",
        color = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom"
      )
  })
  
  # Summary table
  output$summary_table <- renderTable({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "No data available"))
    
    data %>%
      group_by(season.x) %>%
      summarise(
        `Avg Egg Count` = round(mean(egg_count), 1),
        `Avg Larvae Count` = round(mean(larvae_count), 1),
        `Max Egg Count` = max(egg_count),
        `Max Larvae Count` = max(larvae_count),
        `Avg Nitrogen (mg/L)` = round(mean(nitrogen_level), 2),
        `Total Observations` = n()
      ) %>%
      rename(Season = season.x)
  }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, bordered = TRUE)
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
